name: Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail
name_original: Campa de Tres Bispos desde Castelo de Doiras — circular por Sierra de Los Ancares
description: This difficult 22.0-mile mountain biking route from Castelo de Doiras explores the Sierra de Los Ancares, gaining 3622 feet.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cervantes are primarily situated within a mountainous region characterized by river valleys and protected natural areas. The terrain features significant elevation changes, with routes often traversing through dense woodlands and offering views of peaks and conservation zones. This area provides a challenging environment for mountain biking, with a focus on natural, technical trails. Best mountain bike trails around Cervantes The most popular mountain bike trail is Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia, a 19.3 miles (31.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a circular path along the Navia River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel, a difficult 32.7 miles (52.6 km) path. This route explores the Ancares-Courel Special Conservation Area, featuring substantial climbs. Local mountain bikers also love the Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop Trail, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Cervantes is defined by river valleys, protected conservation areas, and significant elevation gains. The network offers options primarily for experienced riders, with 11 of the 14 routes rated as difficult. The routes in Cervantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cervantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cervantes?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cervantes are primarily situated in a mountainous region characterized by river valleys and protected natural areas. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes often traversing through dense woodlands. The terrain is natural and technical, providing a challenging environment for mountain biking.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cervantes?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Cervantes. The majority of these, 11 out of 14, are rated as difficult, indicating a challenging experience for riders.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate mountain bike trails in Cervantes?
A: While most trails in Cervantes are rated difficult, there are 3 moderate trails available for mountain bikers. There are currently no trails rated as easy in this guide.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see while mountain biking in Cervantes?
A: Along your mountain biking routes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Statue at Alto de San Roque&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/383799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real in O Cebreiro&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three passes of O Cebreiro&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4747008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galicia Border Marker on the Camino de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cervantes?
A: The mountain bike routes in Cervantes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Reviewers often praise the challenging nature of the trails, the significant elevation gains, and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and conservation areas.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cervantes?
A: Yes, several routes in Cervantes are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210654475/paradela-y-penamil-desde-a-pobra-circular-del-rio-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt;, which offers substantial climbs through a conservation area.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Cervantes?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in this guide is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 63.7 kilometers (39.6 miles) and involves over 2,200 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Cervantes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Given that 11 out of 14 routes are rated as difficult and feature significant elevation changes and technical terrain, the trails around Cervantes are generally more suited for experienced riders. There are 3 moderate trails, but no easy trails, so families with young children or absolute beginners might find these routes challenging.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Cervantes?
A: The duration for trails varies depending on their length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210654475/paradela-y-penamil-desde-a-pobra-circular-del-rio-navia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela y Penamil desde A Pobra — circular del río Navia&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through special conservation areas?
A: Yes, several trails traverse protected conservation areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211455428/as-nogais-desde-piedrafita-circular-por-la-zona-especial-de-conservacion-los-ancares-courel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Nogais desde Piedrafita — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación Los Ancares-Courel&lt;/a&gt; explores the Ancares-Courel Special Conservation Area, offering unique natural beauty.

Q: What is the highest elevation gain on a single mountain bike trail in Cervantes?
A: The trail with the highest elevation gain is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210426493/puerta-de-ancares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Ancares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features an impressive ascent of over 2,200 meters (7,200 feet).
tips: Imposing defensive the Castelo de Doiras is a beautiful heritage site overlooking the valley road.
An incredible high mountain gravel route through the Sierra de Los Ancares passing the Campa de Tres Bispos before descending into the valley below. The views are spectacular.

faqs.question: Where does the mountain bike trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail, known as "Campa de Tres Bispos desde Castelo de Doiras — circular por Sierra de Los Ancares," typically starts near Doiras Castle. While specific parking details aren't provided, starting points for circular routes in this region usually have designated or informal parking areas nearby. It's advisable to check local information for the most convenient parking options.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route is approximately 35.3 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 1,100 meters. It typically takes around 3 hours and 18 minutes to complete, though individual times may vary based on fitness and skill level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. It begins with a paved section, transitioning to dirt tracks, and then becomes a singletrack path. Riders will encounter significant steep ascents and descents, navigating through gorse, heather, and oak sprouts. Depending on the season, sections can be muddy or wet. The descent through a large holly forest (acebal) is particularly steep, and some parts may require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What skill level is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. It requires very good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills due to its steep climbs, technical descents, and varied terrain. It is not suitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by Doiras Castle at the start. The route offers breathtaking views of the Ancares Mountains, including prominent peaks like Mustallar, Corno Maldito, and Penarrubia. The Campa de Tres Bispos itself is a historical meeting point, and a particularly enchanting section is known as "A Vereda dos Gnomos" (The Gnomes' Path) through a dense holly forest.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is particularly beautiful, as the Campa de Tres Bispos transforms with vibrant yellow narcissus. However, depending on the season, the path can be muddy or wet. Autumn also offers stunning scenery with diverse forest colors. Always check local weather conditions before heading out, as the mountain environment can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Los Ancares is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular mountain biking trail are not detailed. As a general rule in natural parks and biosphere reserves, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users and grazing livestock. Please check with local authorities for precise rules.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Los Ancares?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Los Ancares is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, recognized for its ecological value. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees for accessing the natural park for activities like mountain biking. However, it's always recommended to respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles. No specific permits for mountain biking this route are mentioned.

faqs.question: What wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail immerses you in the rich biodiversity of the Sierra de Los Ancares. You'll ride through diverse forests featuring pine, oak, holly, and maple trees. In spring, the Campa de Tres Bispos is known for its vibrant display of yellow narcissus. You might also encounter local fauna, including cows grazing in the pastures, and the region is home to various other species typical of mountain environments.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 4% of the route overlaps with the official trail known as "[CIMA LU07] O Portelo - Penas do Comeal".

faqs.question: What is an insider tip for tackling this challenging mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike, especially on the very steep descent through the large holly forest (acebal). While challenging, this section, along with "A Vereda dos Gnomos," is also one of the most picturesque and memorable parts of the trail, so embrace the effort for the rewarding scenery.
